batch

Class DisplayMetadata

  • java.lang.Object
    • batch.DisplayMetadata

  • public final class DisplayMetadata
extends java.lang.Object
    Utility class to print media metadata in a format emulating the output style of exiftool -G1 -a -s -u. This class coordinates file discovery through a MetadataScanner, displays file system attributes under the standard [System] group, and renders metadata from supported image formats in a column-aligned view.
    Since:
    2 June 2026
    Version:
    1.1
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs

      private MetadataScanner scanner

      Constructors 
      Constructor Description
      DisplayMetadata(BatchConfiguration config)
      Creates an instance for displaying metadata name/value attributes, similar to the output format produced by exiftool -G1 -a -s -u.

      Executes the metadata extraction pipeline for all matching records discovered by the scanner.

      Formats an epoch timestamp as an ExifTool-style date/time string.
      private java.lang.String getDisplayName(DirectoryIdentifier dir, Taggable tag)
      Applies cosmetic display label transformations to match unique ExifTool output styles.

    • Constructor Detail

      • DisplayMetadata

        public DisplayMetadata(BatchConfiguration config)
        Creates an instance for displaying metadata name/value attributes, similar to the output format produced by exiftool -G1 -a -s -u.
        Parameters:
        config - the configuration containing the validated source parameters and filters supplied on the command line

    • Method Detail

      • execute

        public void execute()
        Executes the metadata extraction pipeline for all matching records discovered by the scanner.

      • displaySystemMetadata

        private void displaySystemMetadata(java.nio.file.Path path)
                            throws java.io.IOException
        Displays file system attributes for the specified path using the standard [System] metadata group.
        Parameters:
        path - the file whose attributes are to be displayed
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if the file system attributes cannot be read

      • formatTimestamp

        private java.lang.String formatTimestamp(long millis)
        Formats an epoch timestamp as an ExifTool-style date/time string.
        Parameters:
        millis - the timestamp in milliseconds since the Unix epoch
        Returns:
        a string in the format yyyy:MM:dd HH:mm:ss±HH:mm, using the system default time zone

      • displayTifMetadata

        private void displayTifMetadata(TifMetadataProvider tif)
        Displays metadata contained within a TIFF-based metadata structure.

        This method iterates through each IFD directory, translates tag identifiers and values into human-readable form, and prints them using ExifTool-style group names. The metadata may originate from a native TIFF file or from TIFF-based EXIF data embedded in formats such as JPEG or HEIC.

        Parameters:
        tif - the metadata provider supplying TIFF directories and associated data

      • displayPngMetadata

        private void displayPngMetadata(PngMetadataProvider pngMeta)

      • getDisplayName

        private java.lang.String getDisplayName(DirectoryIdentifier dir,
                                        Taggable tag)
        Applies cosmetic display label transformations to match unique ExifTool output styles.
